title: Hayden, Rollins, and third Suffolk DA candidate Champion secure enough signatures to appear on ballot
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kevin Hayden, Rachael Rollins, and Linda Champion qualified for Suffolk DA primary ballot with over 1,000 verified signatures each.
In campaign finances, Kevin Hayden has over $60,000, Rachael Rollins over $42,000, and Linda Champion close to $39,000.
Hayden, facing policing scrutiny, is challenged by former DA Rollins and ex-leadership team member Champion in the primary.

title: These are Boston’s highest-paid tech CEOs, BBJ data shows
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Wayfair CEO Niraj Shah received $280.6 million in stock awards, making him the highest-paid Boston tech CEO in 2025.
Vincent Roche of Analog Devices and Neil Barua of PTC Inc. were the second and third highest-paid tech CEOs, respectively.
The Boston Business Journal ranked tech CEOs using stock awards, options, bonuses, and other compensation data from 2025.

title: Karen Read’s SUV heads to auction in Woburn. Here’s what readers think it’s worth.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Karen Read's 2021 Lexus LX 570, linked to a high-profile case, will be auctioned on May 14 in Woburn.
Reader predictions for the SUV's auction price range from market value to over $500,000 due to its notoriety.
Mechanics resolved issues like a dead battery and mouse nest, while its stormy history may impact auction value.

title: Shooting on Memorial Drive in Cambridge leaves ‘at least’ one wounded, police say
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A police-involved shooting occurred on Memorial Drive in Cambridge, with at least one person injured Monday afternoon.
The suspect is in custody, and there is no ongoing threat, according to Harvard University Police Department.
Cambridge police responded to 911 calls about gunshots near River Street at approximately 1:30 p.m.

title: Police investigating viral video of apparent food contamination at McDonald’s in Mass.
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Southbridge police are investigating a viral video allegedly showing a McDonald's employee tampering with food by repackaging touched fries.
The incident at the Southbridge McDonald's prompted collaboration with the Board of Health and restaurant management to assess food safety.
Franchise owners have terminated those involved, emphasizing that the actions don't align with McDonald's safety standards or values.

title: MassINC poll: Many Bay Staters say their quality of life is slipping in Mass.
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: MassINC poll finds rising economic pessimism among Massachusetts residents, with many feeling their quality of life has worsened.
65% of Massachusetts residents aged 35-44 say housing is unaffordable, highlighting economic strains on middle-class families.
Less than a third believe their finances will improve next year; only 27% feel better off than their parents.

title: Celtics reportedly tried to land Giannis Antetokounmpo at the trade deadline
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Boston Celtics reportedly attempted to trade for Giannis Antetokounmpo during the February trade deadline, according to ESPN.
The Bucks are considering trades for Antetokounmpo, with multiple teams including the Celtics showing interest.
Boston faces key challenges and may need additional talent after recent playoff disappointments despite winning a title in 2024.

title: Student charged in Danvers home invasion murder appears in court
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Anthony DeMayo, 18, was arraigned remotely from Bridgewater State Hospital on murder and home invasion charges.
The not guilty plea was entered on DeMayo's behalf; he remains held without bail after being deemed incompetent to stand trial.
Prosecutors successfully requested an independent competency evaluation following his arrest for the March murder of Janet Swallow.

title: Mass. Senate passes bill to protect immigrants, with notable differences from House version
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Massachusetts Senate passes immigration bill with a 37-3 vote, differing from the House version.
Senate version restricts federal warrantless arrests at sensitive locations and creates new civil liabilities for agents.
House version includes rules for correctional facilities and allows some 287(g) agreements for criminal public safety.

title: Kevin Garnett, Paul Pierce analyze what went wrong with ‘robotic’ Celtics offense
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Kevin Garnett and Paul Pierce criticized the Celtics' heavy reliance on three-point shots, calling it predictable and lacking adjustment.
The Celtics, led by Joe Mazzulla, struggled with playoff performance, losing series despite regular season success since 2024.
The departures of key players like Kristaps Porzingis weakened Boston's inside scoring options, impacting their offensive strategy.
